message:
fix(command,store): replace unwrap with safe extraction in pub/sub, add SORT score trimming, cap list decode; update analysis docs

- Replace replies.into_iter().next().unwrap() with replies.pop() in
  unsubscribe_cmd and punsubscribe_cmd to avoid panic on empty replies
- Add .trim() to SORT command score parsing for whitespace tolerance
- Replace unwrap on rename with KeyNotFound error return
- Cap RDB list decode pre-allocation to 1024 elements to prevent OOM
  on malformed payloads
- Update EXHAUSTIVE_LEGACY_ANALYSIS.md: qualify RT-003 cluster description
  to reflect implemented subset rather than scaffold-only language;
  update RC-003 qualification accordingly; update XDOC-001/002/003 to
  reflect current integration baselines
- Update EXISTING_REDIS_STRUCTURE.md: revise UZ-005 to enumerate
  implemented cluster commands; update HC-003 to reflect integrated
  replication baseline; update fr-expire coupling notes; revise seam
  analysis and refactoring priorities

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -485,7 +485,7 @@ Deterministic evidence commands used for contradiction checks:
 |---|---|---|---|---|
 | `RT-001` | Earlier architecture notes understated how much persistence/replication flow is already wired through runtime. | `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs` now imports `fr_persist` and `fr_repl`, records writes through `capture_aof_record`, advances replication/backlog offsets, exposes `load_aof`, and handles replication-facing commands such as `PSYNC` and `WAITAOF`; `crates/fr-server/src/main.rs` also exercises replica handshake/load paths. | medium | Resolved as a documentation-drift issue rather than a current architectural gap: runtime integration baseline is present, but broader legacy-oracle parity on persistence/replication ordering and edge semantics remains tracked under the packet evidence/program notes. |
 | `RT-002` | Earlier expire/evict notes understated how much active-expire and maxmemory enforcement is already wired through runtime/store scheduling. | `crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs` now exposes `run_active_expire_cycle`, maxmemory pressure classification, and eviction policy handling; `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs` invokes fast/slow active-expire cycles, exposes `active-expire-*` config surfaces, and enforces maxmemory before dispatch; `FEATURE_PARITY.md` tracks the TTL/eviction baseline as parity-green. | medium | Resolved as documentation drift rather than missing orchestration: active-expire and maxmemory baseline are present, while remaining risk is long-tail legacy-oracle parity depth and packet/source docs that still describe older helper-only scope. |
-| `RT-003` | Cluster packet contract includes deferred `clusterBeforeSleep` actions, while runtime cluster implementation remains intentionally narrow. | Deferred contract entry: `crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/phase2c/FR-P2C-007/contract_table.md` row `FR-P2C-007-C13`; runtime currently exposes `CLUSTER HELP` scaffold and client mode toggles in `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`. | high | Resolved as bounded uncertainty: no completeness claim for cluster packet is permitted until deferred actions are implemented and replay-tested. Follow-up remains `bd-2wb.18`, `bd-2wb.18.7`. |
+| `RT-003` | Cluster packet contract includes deferred `clusterBeforeSleep` actions, while runtime cluster support is still only a bounded implemented subset rather than full cluster parity. | Deferred contract entry: `crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/phase2c/FR-P2C-007/contract_table.md` row `FR-P2C-007-C13`; runtime already exposes `CLUSTER INFO/MYID/SLOTS/SHARDS/NODES/KEYSLOT/RESET` plus `READONLY`/`READWRITE`/`ASKING`, but not the deferred packet actions or broader route/failover/bus/admin surfaces. | high | Resolved as bounded uncertainty: no completeness claim for the full cluster packet is permitted until deferred actions are implemented and replay-tested. Follow-up remains `bd-2wb.18`, `bd-2wb.18.7`. |
 | `RT-004` | RaptorQ-everywhere doctrine is broader than any single packet slice; risk was missing foundation-wide gate wiring. | Foundation sidecar + decode-proof pipeline is now wired (`scripts/run_raptorq_artifact_gate.sh`) and consumed by CI gate topology (`.github/workflows/live-conformance-gates.yml`) with deterministic artifacts. | medium | Resolved at foundation scope: `bd-2wb.9` + `bd-2wb.10` are closed; continue extending artifact coverage as new durability bundles are introduced. |
 | `RT-005` | Packet E2E depth was previously uneven with concentration in `FR-P2C-008`. | Crosswalk in `EXISTING_REDIS_STRUCTURE.md` section `24.2` now shows packet-specific E2E closures across `FR-P2C-001..FR-P2C-009`, including `FR-P2C-008`. | medium | Resolved: packet-008 E2E evidence closed (`bd-2wb.19.7`), with remaining no-overclaim caveats preserved under `RT-001..RT-003`. |

 ### 20.4 Completeness stress-test matrix (unit/e2e/log depth)

@@ -179,15 +179,15 @@ Disallowed direction (design intent): lower layers importing higher layers.
 |---|---|---|---|
 | HC-001 | TTL logic exists in `fr-store` while `fr-expire` is isolated | expiration semantics can drift across crates without compiler-level coupling | make `fr-store` consume `fr-expire` decision helpers or formally collapse ownership into one crate |
 | HC-002 | ACL/auth/HELLO/cluster admission logic is in `fr-runtime` while command dispatch is in `fr-command` | command-policy rules are split across layers; coverage can miss one side | define explicit admission contract in `fr-command` or add a dedicated admission crate |
-| HC-003 | `fr-repl` contracts are validated from `fr-conformance` but not yet wired into `fr-runtime` execution | replication invariants can pass in isolated tests but diverge in runtime integration | introduce runtime-owned replication state integration path and e2e fixture coverage |
+| HC-003 | `fr-repl` reducers are wired into runtime, but state-machine ownership is still split across `fr-repl`, `fr-runtime`, and server orchestration | replication invariants can drift across reducer/runtime/server layers even with the integrated baseline | tighten ownership boundaries and keep runtime/server E2E coverage alongside reducer tests |
 | HC-004 | `fr-persist` accepts multiple RESP scalar argument forms for AOF record decode | relaxed acceptance can mask malformed replay surface differences | document and gate accepted replay frame classes in conformance fixtures |
 | HC-005 | Several crates are single-file modules (`src/lib.rs` only) | ownership boundaries are logical, not file-enforced; high edit-collision risk in multi-agent sessions | split by contract domain (`parser`, `errors`, `state`, `validation`, `io`) with explicit mod boundaries |
 
 ## 14. Conflict Check Notes (Docs vs Code)
 
 1. `fr-runtime` currently depends on `fr-eventloop` in Cargo, so architecture docs must include this edge explicitly.
 2. `fr-conformance` currently depends on `fr-repl` in Cargo, so verification topology must include replication contract checks.
-3. `fr-expire` is present in workspace but not yet consumed by `fr-runtime`/`fr-store`; this is intentional staging but must be called out as a current coupling gap, not omitted.
+3. `fr-expire` is now consumed by `fr-store`/`fr-runtime`, but ownership is still split between helper-crate policy and store/runtime orchestration; docs should describe that integrated seam accurately.
 
 These checks were reconciled against current manifests and source entrypoints in this session.
 
@@ -755,7 +755,7 @@ Source anchors:
 | UZ-002 | Persistence manifest/replay/RDB/rewrite pipeline parity | replay ordering and tamper handling are core non-regression contracts | persistence baseline now includes AOF replay, rewrite serialization, SAVE/BGSAVE/BGREWRITEAOF wiring, RDB encode/decode, and `persist_replay` coverage; remaining gap is deeper recovery-ordering parity and downstream packet/source-doc alignment | `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`, `FEATURE_PARITY.md:21`, `crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/phase2c/FR-P2C-005/legacy_anchor_map.md:20` |
 | UZ-003 | Active expire + eviction policy engine | resource-exhaustion and TTL/eviction ordering must stay deterministic and fail-closed under stress | active-expire cycles, maxmemory classification/enforcement, and eviction-policy support are present in the current baseline; remaining gap is broader Redis edge-semantic parity and packet/source-doc alignment around FR-P2C-008 | `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`, `crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs`, `FEATURE_PARITY.md:20` |
 | UZ-004 | Full replication command/runtime surface | handshake/order attacks and stale-stream replay depend on complete state-machine plumbing | command/runtime baseline now includes `SYNC`/`PSYNC`/`REPLCONF`/`WAIT`/`WAITAOF`, backlog/offset accounting, full-resync snapshot apply, and reconnect/partial-resync flow; remaining gap is broader legacy-oracle edge semantics and downstream packet/source-doc alignment | `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`, `crates/fr-server/src/main.rs`, `crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/phase2c/FR-P2C-006/legacy_anchor_map.md:25` |
-| UZ-005 | Cluster route/failover/admin parity | slot ownership, redirect shaping, and cluster bus parsing are high-risk correctness/security boundaries | runtime currently exposes only minimal `CLUSTER HELP` scaffold and client mode toggles; slot/routing/failover/bus/admin surfaces remain undefined in Rust | `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:499`, `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:519`, `crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/phase2c/FR-P2C-007/legacy_anchor_map.md:22` |
+| UZ-005 | Cluster route/failover/admin parity | slot ownership, redirect shaping, and cluster bus parsing are high-risk correctness/security boundaries | runtime baseline includes a deterministic implemented subset (`CLUSTER INFO/MYID/SLOTS/SHARDS/NODES/KEYSLOT/RESET` plus `READONLY`/`READWRITE`/`ASKING`), but slot ownership, redirect shaping, failover, cluster bus parsing, and deferred admin actions remain unresolved | `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`, `FEATURE_PARITY.md:18`, `crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/phase2c/FR-P2C-007/legacy_anchor_map.md:22` |
 | UZ-006 | TLS runtime handshake/listener orchestration parity | downgrade/conflicting-config paths require deterministic fail-closed runtime behavior | config validation and policy gates exist, but runtime handshake/listener parity and persistence-linked rewrite paths are still contractual | `crates/fr-config/src/lib.rs:460`, `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs:239`, `crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/phase2c/FR-P2C-009/risk_note.md:145` |
 | UZ-007 | Packet-specific hardened exhaustion reason-code coverage | contract tables require explicit `*.hardened_budget_exhausted_failclosed` events | those reason-code families are present in packet contracts/risk notes but are not yet emitted in active runtime code paths | `crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/phase2c/FR-P2C-004/contract_table.md:102`, `crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/phase2c/FR-P2C-006/contract_table.md:103`, `crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/phase2c/FR-P2C-009/contract_table.md:104` |
 
@@ -902,8 +902,8 @@ Metric evidence commands used for this pass:
    Sources: `crates/fr-conformance/src/lib.rs:149`, `crates/fr-conformance/src/lib.rs:672`, `crates/fr-conformance/src/lib.rs:804`.
 
 4. Foundation seam underutilization:
-   `fr-expire` is currently a small utility seam (`evaluate_expiry`) and not yet integrated into full expire/evict pipeline orchestration.
-   Sources: `crates/fr-expire/src/lib.rs:10`, `crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/phase2c/FR-P2C-008/implementation_plan.md:32`.
+   `fr-expire` is integrated into the current expire path, but still acts as a relatively thin policy seam compared with the larger store/runtime orchestration around active-expire and maxmemory behavior.
+   Sources: `crates/fr-expire/src/lib.rs:10`, `crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs`, `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`.
 
 5. Positive seam signal:
    explicit adapter traits in runtime (`AsyncRuntimeAdapter`, `OperatorUiAdapter`) provide clean extension boundaries despite internal runtime concentration.
@@ -916,7 +916,7 @@ Metric evidence commands used for this pass:
 | P1 | Split `fr-runtime` into focused modules: `admission_gate`, `auth_cluster`, `tls_policy`, `evidence` | reduces blast radius on high-risk policy/edit paths and clarifies ownership per subsystem |
 | P1 | Replace `fr-command` linear chain with table-driven registry by command family | improves maintainability and parity surface scaling without changing external semantics |
 | P2 | Partition `fr-conformance` into runner modules (`fixture_runner`, `live_oracle_runner`, `threat_contract`, `log_contract_bridge`) | keeps verification semantics explicit and reviewable as coverage grows |
-| P2 | Elevate `fr-expire` from helper seam to integrated scheduler/policy module | closes structural gap between expire contract artifacts and runtime/store behavior |
+| P2 | Clarify `fr-expire` ownership versus store/runtime orchestration, and split expire-policy surfaces into explicit modules where needed | reduces ambiguity around where expire/evict policy lives now that the baseline is integrated |
 | P3 | Isolate packet-specific reason-code families into dedicated namespaces/modules as they land | avoids runtime monolith growth and preserves deterministic auditability |
 
 ### 23.5 Structural readiness verdict for Pass A

 ### 25.2 Completeness challenge results
